conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child, Alex, who's 15 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility, I need to ask: 

Are you or your partner currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex from anyone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

Does Alex live with you full-time, or do they spend time living with someone else (for example, another parent or guardian)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're the one who looks after them and pays for things like their food, clothing, and housing.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I look after them and pay for all their needs like food, clothes, and everything else they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (This would be relevant since Alex is 15 years old)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One more important question:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in the care of local authority services (such as being looked after by the council)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or in care - they're just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I need to clarify one important point:

You mentioned that Alex is 15 years old and not currently in any education or training. For children aged 16 or over, they generally need to be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit. However, since Alex is still 15, I need to ask:

Is Alex still under 16 years old (meaning they haven't had their 16th birthday yet)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is still 15, hasn't turned 16 yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming all the details. Let me also ask one final question to complete the assessment:

Are you living in the UK, or are you otherwise eligible to claim UK benefits (for example, through working in the UK or having a relevant immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16), so they qualify for Child Benefit regardless of whether they're in education or training
- You're responsible for Alex - you look after them and pay for their needs
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or local authority care

You meet all the eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules. You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives full-time with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and pays for their needs. The claimant lives in the UK and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is under 16, they qualify regardless of whether they are in education or training. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant is responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them full-time in the UK, no one else is claiming for this child, and the child is not in hospital or care. All the key eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
